entry.title: Hello SAP Community! My name is Gurram Dhanunjay Reddy and I am passionate about learning SAP
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community! I'm Gurram Dhanunjaya Reddy, and I'm a student studying enterprise technologies and SAP. I'm curious about how companies handle production, supply chains, and business operations using programs like SAP.

I have been investigating various SAP modules and ideas throughout my studies, including enterprise resource planning systems, business process management, and production planning. I like discovering how technology can boost corporate productivity and address actual business issues.

Here are the questions I’ve chosen to answer as a part of the "Hello SAP Community" initiative.

What is your professional life dream?

My career goal is to work as a proficient SAP consultant or technology specialist who can assist businesses in streamlining their operations. I want to assist businesses in their digital transformation by working with cutting-edge enterprise systems. Being a part of that change would mean a lot to me because I think SAP technologies are crucial to modern businesses.

Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

I envision myself working in the technology or enterprise software sector and developing a solid grasp of SAP systems within the next five years. I want to work on practical projects where I can put my technical expertise to use and keep learning about new technologies like artificial intelligence, cloud computing, and data analytics. My objective is to develop my ability to use technology to solve challenging business issues.

What is your hobby?

Developing my technical skills and learning about new technologies are two of my favourite pastimes. I like experimenting with business systems, programming, and problem-solving exercises. I occasionally enjoy reading about technological advancements and how they are influencing various industries.

What is the best lesson your parents taught you?

The importance of perseverance and hard work is among the most significant lessons my parents instilled in me. They consistently inspired me to never give up in the face of difficulties and to never stop learning. This way of thinking keeps me motivated for both my academic work and personal growth.

What do you imagine is the future of technology topics like AI and Cloud ?

Technologies like cloud computing and artificial intelligence, in my opinion, will be crucial to businesses in the future. A lot of businesses are already shifting their systems to the cloud and utilising AI for data analysis and better decision-making. Businesses will become more automated, data-driven, and efficient in the future thanks to these technologies. Professionals who are familiar with these technologies will, in my opinion, have a lot of opportunities.

My Experience with SAP TechEd Bangalore

 

I had the chance to attend SAP TechEd Bangalore 2026 this year, and it was a really worthwhile and thrilling experience for me. SAP experts, developers, students, and tech professionals from various locations came together for the event to exchange ideas and knowledge about the most recent advancements in SAP.

I had the opportunity to attend a number of sessions and presentations during the event where specialists discussed emerging trends and technologies within the SAP ecosystem. Many sessions focused on topics such as SAP S/4HANA, cloud technologies, artificial intelligence, and digital transformation. Seeing how these technologies are assisting businesses in streamlining their operations and increasing productivity was fascinating.

 

And now let’s hear from you!

I would love to hear your thoughts.

Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

Feel free to share your answer in the comments and tell us about your goals and dreams.

entry.title: Re: Why is help.sap.com not accessible to ai agents?
entry.content: Sometimes documentation sites like SAP Help Portal may not be accessible to certain AI tools because of access restrictions, authentication requirements, or bot-blocking mechanisms. Some platforms limit automated requests to protect their documentation and servers. In many cases, the content is still accessible through a normal browser or through official APIs if integration is allowed. Hopefully SAP or the platform team can clarify whether it’s a temporary restriction or a policy related to AI access.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Delete Parked Document for Supplier Down Pa...
entry.content:
Hello Rohini,

@rohini_dutta21 

The parked supplier down payment cannot be deleted while the workflow instance is active. The workflow must first be cancelled or completed before the parked document can be removed.

 

Scenario / QuestionExplanation, Standard Behavior & SAP Notes
Down Payment Request created in February (Doc Type KA)In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, supplier down payment requests created through workflow-based apps are parked until workflow approval is completed. The final posting step occurs automatically via background job.
Workflow approvals completed in MarchWhen workflow finishes, the system attempts to post the accounting document using the original posting date stored in the parked document (February).
Posting period February closedSince period 002/2026 is closed, the background posting fails with error: Posting period not open. The workflow remains in error status.
Why opening the period in QAS did not resolve itIn workflow-managed documents, the posting attempt may still use the original posting context or cached workflow state. Simply opening the period afterward may not re-trigger the posting automatically.
Can the parked document be deleted?❌ Direct deletion is generally not allowed while the document is part of an active workflow instance. Workflow must be completed or cancelled first.
Standard solution option 1Cancel the workflow instance in the Manage Workflows or Flexible Workflow Administration app (depending on configuration). Once the workflow is terminated, the parked document can be deleted.
Standard solution option 2Use the Manage Supplier Down Payment Requests or Display Parked Documents app to reset or delete the parked document after the workflow is cancelled.
Alternative workaroundIf business wants to keep the document: 1️⃣ Temporarily reopen the posting period 2️⃣ Restart or repost the workflow step 3️⃣ Allow posting to complete 4️⃣ Close the period again.
Important system behaviorIn Public Cloud, workflow-managed financial documents cannot bypass workflow control, meaning manual deletion or posting through backend transactions is not available.
SAP Help ReferenceSAP Help Portal → Supplier Down Payment Processing SAP Help → Flexible Workflow for Accounts Payable


If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Issue with Balance Carryforward and Legacy Data Trans...
entry.content:
Hello @efsatug94 ,

In S/4HANA, P&L accounts are not physically reset to zero. Instead:

-balances move to retained earnings account
-reports calculate zero opening balance logically

Therefore some trial balance layouts may still show cumulative movements.

 

Question / SettingExplanation, Recommended Value & SAP Notes
P&L balances appearing in 2026 Trial BalanceIn S/4HANA, P&L accounts are not physically cleared during balance carryforward. Instead, the system calculates opening balances dynamically. If settings related to Legacy Data Transfer (LDT) or fiscal year closing are incomplete, P&L balances may appear cumulative.
Legacy Data Transfer Segment – Transfer StatusAfter completing all migration postings and reconciliation, Transfer Status should be set to “Completed.” This indicates that the legacy migration phase is finished and allows normal financial closing processes to proceed correctly.
Highest Fiscal YearThis field should reflect the latest fiscal year in which postings exist. Since your closing process concerns FY2025 and postings are occurring in 2026, the Highest Fiscal Year should be set to 2026.
Closed Fiscal YearThis value represents the latest fully closed fiscal year for Asset Accounting. Because your system started with LDT in 2025, the Closed Fiscal Year should normally be 2024 (the year prior to migration).
Sequence for Balance CarryforwardProper sequence is critical: 1️⃣ Ensure LDT Transfer Status = Completed 2️⃣ Confirm Closed Fiscal Year = 2024 3️⃣ Confirm Highest Fiscal Year = 2026 4️⃣ Run Balance Carryforward (app equivalent of FAGLGV)
Why P&L accounts are not zero in reportsTrial Balance may display cumulative values depending on reporting variant. In S/4HANA Universal Journal, P&L clearing happens logically via retained earnings, not by clearing individual accounts.
Impact of incorrect LDT settingsIf Transfer Status remains “Ongoing,” the system assumes migration is still active and may block proper year-end processing, including balance carryforward behavior.
Where to check settingsApp: Make Company Code Settings – Asset Accounting Specific
SAP Help ReferenceSAP Help Portal → Balance Carryforward in S/4HANA Cloud SAP Help → Legacy Data Transfer in Finance Migration


If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: House Bank Currency Showing Incorrectly During Bank-t...
entry.content:
Hello Rohini,

@rohini_dutta21 

This behavior is generally standard, as the app initially derives the transfer currency from the company code currency, not strictly from the bank account currency.

How Currency Determination Works in the App

Priority logic generally follows:

a. Company Code Currency
b. Transaction Currency entered in the transfer app
c. Bank Account Currency

If different currencies exist, SAP automatically performs exchange rate conversion during posting.

Verify:-

-Verify company code currency (likely GBP).
-Confirm bank account currency = USD in Manage Bank Accounts.
-Ensure the bank GL account currency is not restricted to GBP.
-Try selecting USD manually in the currency field — the system should allow it.

 

Scenario / QuestionExplanation, Root Cause & SAP Notes
Bank account maintained with USD currencyThe currency maintained in Bank Account Management (house bank account) defines the actual posting currency of the bank GL account.
In Make Bank Transfer app the currency defaults to GBPThis field is usually derived from the Company Code Currency, not directly from the bank account currency. If your company code currency is GBP, the transfer amount field will initially default to GBP.
Why system behaves this wayThe Make Bank Transfer app posts accounting documents in company code currency context first, then performs currency translation if the bank account is maintained in a different currency.
What happens when posting is executedIf the receiving or paying bank account is maintained in USD, the system automatically performs currency conversion between company code currency (GBP) and bank account currency (USD) during posting.
When USD should appear insteadIf both: • Paying bank account currency = USD • Receiving bank account currency = USD then the transaction currency should default to USD.
Check configurationVerify the following: ✔ Bank Account Currency in Manage Bank Accounts ✔ Company Code Currency in Define Company Code ✔ Bank GL account currency in Chart of Accounts / G/L master
Important behavior in Public CloudThe Amount Currency field represents transaction currency, which can differ from bank account currency. The app allows multi-currency transfers.
Possible cause in your caseSince the company code IQ04 likely has GBP as company code currency, the system defaults the transfer currency to GBP even though the bank account is maintained in USD.
SAP Help ReferenceSAP Help Portal → Bank Account Transfers in S/4HANA Cloud SAP Help → Manage Bank Accounts and House Banks

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Homogeneous DMO and DMOVE2S4
entry.content:
Hi Rajewsari/Boris,

We are planning a conversion and migration from an on-premise ECC on HANA system to S/4HANA 2025 FPS00 in RISE (Hyperscaler – AWS, Migration Method-DMOVE2S4 ).

Once the Development system has been successfully migrated to RISE, we would like to reuse the existing on-premise Development system for emergency production transports until the Production system is converted and migrated to RISE. This interim period is expected to be approximately six months following the Development system migration.

As part of the migration to RISE, we will be using a Migration Server to run the AAS and SUM processes.

Could you please advise on the best approach to handle this scenario? Specifically, we would like to understand whether the on-premise Development system can still be reused after the conversion/migration, or if it would be necessary to rebuild the Development system in order to use it for emergency production transports within the existing production landscape.

Thank you in advance for your guidance.

Regards,

Heinz

entry.title: [South Korea] DRC Activation and License Usage
entry.content:
Currently, the system is designed to allow activation by country. Since we have two legal entities in Korea, the system is flagging this as excessive license usage (over-usage).

To resolve this, we intend to activate and use DRC for only one of the two entities. However, we are unsure of the specific steps to configure this at the entity level rather than the country level.

Could you please provide guidance or documentation on how to limit the activation to a single legal entity?

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: [South Korea] DRC Activation and License Usage
entry.content:
Dear customer,

 

Thank you for reaching out. To proceed with your request, there are two steps that need to be completed:    

  1. Ensure that SSCUI 105518 (Activate Document and Reporting Compliance Features ) is activated for South Korea. This step is essential to enable the necessary features for document and reporting compliance.
  2. Once the above activation is complete, you can define the types of source documents for which electronic documents should be created within a company code. This can be achieved using SSCUI 102190 (Activate Source Type Documents for Company Code ). You have the flexibility to define multiple entries for a single company code, depending on your requirements.  

 If you need further clarification or assistance with these steps, I’m here to help. Please let me know how I can support you further.

 

Best regards,

SAP Technical Support, Public Cloud ERP

entry.title: You are not entitled for SAP Note/KBAXXXXXX
entry.content:
Receiving an error message when trying to open any KBA note.
Error message: Sorry! You are not entitled for SAP Note/KBAXXXXXX


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: You are not entitled for SAP Note/KBAXXXXXX
entry.content:
• If you see the Maintenance Terminated warning in a banner when you go to the SAP for Me Home page, click the Learn More hyperlink in the "Maintenance Terminated - Limited Access" message. Basically, to resolve the Maintenance Terminated issue, contact your SAP Account Manager according to KBA 2626344 or email SAP Order Management (contracts team) at sapcontractcenter@sap.com.
  • If you don't see the Maintenance Terminated message on the SAP for Me Home page, make sure you are logged in with your S-user ID (not a P-user).
  • If the issue only occurs on one note, it could be a pilot note which you need to request access to. For example, some pilot KBAs are only available to a specific group of customers, so customers who have not been granted access may encounter the following error message: "You are not entitled for SAP Note/KBAXXXXXX. The target audience for the SAP Note/KBA you were trying to reach does not fit your profile". For information on where to find Pilot Notes, please refer to SAP Note 2388433 - "Information about using Expert Search for SAP Notes & KBAs - SAP for Me."

reference KBA:3233516 - Sorry! You are not entitled to access SAP Note/KBA - SAP for Me - SAP for Me

entry.title: GRC Tuesdays: What's New in SAP GRC for SAP HANA
entry.content:
It’s here, yes, it’s really here! No, not the new iPhone but much better than that. The new SAP GRC for SAP HANA release!

Since March 6th, it is available in restricted shipment – meaning for customers in Early Adoption Care program but, this summer, in Q3, it will be generally available to all customers.

If you don’t believe me, then here is the official SAP Note 3709892 - Release Information Note for SAP GRC For SAP HANA

 

What is SAP GRC for SAP HANA?

 

SAP GRC for SAP HANA is the next evolution of SAP GRC, unifying SAP GRC 12.0 and SAP GRC 12.0 for S/4HANA into a single HANA-based platform for future innovations, and it will continue to be available both OnPremise and in the cloud.  

Built on 20 years of in market expertise, it replaces SAP GRC 12.0 “classic” versions (SAP Access Control, Process Control and Risk Management, UI masking and UI logging) and is the new version for SAP GRC for S/4HANA 12.0 products (including SAP Access Control for S/4HANA, SAP Process Control for S/4HANA and SAP Risk Management for S/4HANA), SAP Assurance and Compliance Software (SAP Business Integrity Screening and SAP Audit Management) and UI masking and UI logging for S/4HANA. 

This evolution delivers a unified platform to manage Governance, Risk, and Compliance requirements across business processes:

Fully cohesive with the SAP Business Suite with its native integration into SAP S/4HANA – the AI ERP, SAP Signavio – the single source of truth for business processes, Regulatory Insights – for regulatory surveillance, and SAP Analytics Cloud – for best-in-class reporting and visualization, data flows from one module to another seamlessly and establishes a glass pane where all risks are surfaced and tracked in a single unified yet modular platform for governance, control, risk management and data protection:

 

What’s new compared to previous versions?

 

SAP GRC for SAP HANA elevates the user experience by shifting from complex, context-heavy interfaces to modern, intuitive designs that prioritize simplicity and efficiency.

New dedicated SAP Fiori UIs, provide a consistent, user-friendly design across different devices and platforms. Boosting adoption and productivity.

Context and role-based, these apps deliver personalized interactions with relevant tiles adapted to user’s profile and needs.

Designed for organizations in all geographies, this new version is truly multilingual with 28 languages supported across all modules.

For Governance aspects, SAP GRC for SAP HANA integrates with SAP Identity Services and Entra ID to establish a single source of truth for user identity, enhancing security, compliance, and operational efficiency.

For Control and Compliance, the platform integrates to the Regulatory Insights service to enable 2nd line users to effortlessly update the control framework based on the latest regulatory information and the new sub-scenario for SAP Integration Suite with pre-delivered integrations extends the connectivity of Continuous Control Monitoring to SAP and non-SAP sources for hybrid landscape.

To increase transparency, security, compliance, and decision-making capabilities, Analytics Dashboard enable business users to evaluate and navigate their specific risk and compliance tasks efficiently and gain additional contextual insights.

The native integration of SAP GRC for SAP HANA to the SAP Analytics Cloud reporting engine also enables best-in-class visualization and reporting. As a next step, SAP GRC for SAP HANA also intends to activate secure and standardised data assets for cloud-based analytics and insights on SAP Business Data Cloud.

UI Data Protection logging and masking modules are now natively embedded in SAP GRC for SAP HANA. Strengthening the protection of data with configurable masking and blocking safeguards sensitive field values across SAP interfaces and secures operations by ensuring only authorized users can view or reveal protected information when needed.

Together, logging and masking reduce data-abuse risks, help organizations meet legal retention and privacy requirements, and empower data protection teams with easy access to meaningful insights.

A study by McKinsey concluded that 88% of companies “report regular AI use in at least one business function” at the end of 2025.

With GRC being at the crossroad of core business processes, multiple AI use cases have been already added to SAP GRC for SAP HANA, and more are planned before the end of the year.

Select examples of what has been delivered but also plans in progress include:

AI in Governance

 

User Augmented Access Management by integrating SAP’s conversational AI agent Joule for user access request processes

 

Key benefits:

·       Faster, more accurate requests with less manual effort

·       Enhanced user experience via AI-driven interaction

·       Improved consistency and compliance with role models

 

 

AI-supported sensitive fields advisor for masking to receive recommendations on personal data fields available in end-user applications, enabling organizations to secure sensitive information across multiple applications without manually collecting field details

 

Key benefits:

·       Increase coverage of sensitive data protection

·       Reduce manual effort, implementation and operational effort

·       Improve solution implementation experience

 

 

AI in Risk Management

 

AI-powered end-user support for key risk indicators and data configuration with Joule.

 

Key benefits:

·       Support KRI implementation and KRI business rule creation using Joule, with AI-driven suggestions for existing CDS views, queries, OData services, and source tables for customer-specific CDS view development.

 

 

AI in Compliance and Assurance

 

Intelligent self-diagnostic cockpit by automatically analysing the errors in continuous control monitoring and proposing resolution capabilities

 

Key benefits:

·       Conduct automated control jobs for self-diagnosing errors and providing solutions

·       Enhance operational efficiency and resilience with issue detection and problem-solving guidance

 

 

AI-supported Audit Report Summary by leveraging prompts to customize the output of the audit report summary

 

Key benefits:

·       Automate the generation of the audit report and customize the outcome

·       Use existing work programs, working papers, and findings as inputs for the AI prompts

 

 

Key take aways

 

SAP GRC for SAP HANA is a new evolution of SAP GRC solutions. This first 2026 release is already filled with enhancements, and more are planned.

Fully integrated into the SAP Business Suite, SAP GRC for SAP HANA creates a single continuously updated platform, automatically monitoring your core processes, governing access and protecting sensitive data.

With SAP GRC for SAP HANA, you can expect to achieve:

 

Early detection of issues and timely closure

 

Improved assurance transparency and compliance process efficiency

 

Data quality checks for finance, compliance and risk management purposes

Oh, and one last thing: keep an eye out for our next blog – Why Move to SAP GRC for SAP HANA which will be released in this same GRC Tuesdays series on March 31st.

Since this blog can’t be exhaustive without being too long, we have taken the liberty of selecting the enhancements that we felt were the most relevant. But many more have been added to SAP GRC for SAP HANA and are detailed on their respective SAP Notes:

  • What’s New in SAP Access Control for SAP HANA
  • What’s New in SAP Process Control for SAP HANA
  • What’s New in SAP Risk Management for SAP HANA
  • What’s New in SAP Audit Management for SAP HANA
  • What’s New in SAP Business Integrity Screening for SAP HANA
  • What’s New in SAP UI Data Protection Logging for SAP HANA
  • What’s New in SAP UI Data Protection Masking for SAP HANA

And if you are interested in learning more about SAP solutions for Governance, Risk, and Compliance, feel free to fill-in the demo request form!

entry.title: Top SQL Queries Every SAP Databricks Admins Should Keep in your Back Pocket
entry.content:
All the SQL queries provided are intended for use with SAP Databricks System Tables. Central to this is the fact that System Tables are centrally stored and governed by Unity Catalog.
 
A solid SQL toolkit can make an SAP Databricks admin unstoppable. Here are the queries you’ll rely on the most—fast, essential, and built for real‑world scenarios.

A reliable set of SQL queries that help you diagnose issues, validate access, and keep your environment running smoothly. This blog highlights the must‑know queries that every admin should bookmark or pin it.

Important Note :

Before executing the SQL queries below, please ensure that you have the required permissions and access to the relevant tables. If not, kindly contact your SAP Databricks Administrator in your organization to request the necessary privileges.

Resource Monitoring - DB SQL Warehouses that are actively running and for how long

This query helps understanding which warehouses are currently active and their running time in hours. 

SELECT
    we.workspace_id,
    we.warehouse_id,
    we.event_time,
    TIMESTAMPDIFF(MINUTE, we.event_time, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP()) / 60.0 AS running_hours,
    we.cluster_count
FROM
    system.compute.warehouse_events we
WHERE
    we.event_type = 'RUNNING'
    AND NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1
        FROM compute.warehouse_events we2
        WHERE we2.warehouse_id = we.warehouse_id
        AND we2.event_time > we.event_time
    )

Resource Monitoring - Warehouses that are upscaled longer than expected

This query identifies warehouses that have scaled up (increased in cluster count) and have remained in that state longer than usual.

SELECT
   we.workspace_id,
   we.warehouse_id,
   we.event_time,
   TIMESTAMPDIFF(MINUTE, we.event_time, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP()) / 60.0 AS upscaled_hours,
   we.cluster_count
FROM
   system.compute.warehouse_events we
WHERE
   we.event_type = 'SCALED_UP'
   AND we.cluster_count >= 2
   AND NOT EXISTS (
       SELECT 1
       FROM compute.warehouse_events we2
       WHERE we2.warehouse_id = we.warehouse_id
       AND (
           (we2.event_type = 'SCALED_DOWN') OR 
           (we2.event_type = 'SCALED_UP' AND we2.cluster_count < 2)
       )
       AND we2.event_time > we.event_time
   )

Join the list_prices table with the usage table

SELECT
 SUM(usage.usage_quantity * list_prices.pricing.effective_list.default)
 as `Total Dollar Cost`
FROM system.billing.usage
JOIN system.billing.list_prices ON list_prices.sku_name = usage.sku_name
WHERE identity_metadata.run_as = {{'sample identity'}}
AND usage.usage_end_time >= list_prices.price_start_time
AND (list_prices.price_end_time IS NULL OR usage.usage_end_time < list_prices.price_end_time)
AND usage.usage_date BETWEEN "2025-04-01" AND "2025-04-15"

What is the daily spend on the different SAP Databricks products?

This query identifies the consumption generated in DBUs and $DBUs at list price differentiated by SKU on a daily basis.

SELECT
    u.usage_date,
    u.sku_name,
    SUM(u.usage_quantity) AS dbus,
    FIRST(lp.pricing.default) AS list_price,
    SUM(u.usage_quantity) * FIRST(lp.pricing.default) AS dollar_dbus_list
FROM system.billing.usage u
INNER JOIN system.billing.list_prices lp ON u.sku_name = lp.sku_name
    AND u.usage_start_time >= lp.price_start_time
    AND (
        u.usage_end_time <= lp.price_end_time
        or lp.price_end_time is null
    )
GROUP BY u.usage_date, u.sku_name
ORDER BY u.usage_date, u.sku_name DESC

What is the spend on the different Databricks products per month per workspace?

This query identifies the consumption generated in DBUs and $DBUs at list price on a monthly basis by every workspace.

SELECT
    LEFT(u.usage_date, 7) AS year_month,
    u.workspace_id,
    u.sku_name,
    SUM(u.usage_quantity) AS dbus,
    FIRST(lp.pricing.default) AS list_price,
    SUM(u.usage_quantity) * FIRST(lp.pricing.default) AS dollar_dbus_list
FROM
    system.billing.usage u
INNER JOIN system.billing.list_prices lp ON u.sku_name = lp.sku_name
    AND u.usage_start_time >= lp.price_start_time
    AND ( u.usage_end_time <= lp.price_end_time OR lp.price_end_time is null)
GROUP BY u.workspace_id, LEFT(u.usage_date, 7), u.sku_name
ORDER BY year_month, u.workspace_id DESC

Suspects for query inefficiency 

The query below shows the potentially most inefficient queries from the last 30 days (based on shuffle).

SELECT
    workspace_id,
    warehouse_id,
    statement_id, 
    statement_text,
    SUM(shuffle_read_bytes) AS shuffle_read_bytes
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time between DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE, 30) AND CURRENT_DATE
GROUP BY workspace_id, warehouse_id, statement_id, statement_text
HAVING shuffle_read_bytes > 0
ORDER BY shuffle_read_bytes DESC;

Identify expensive notebooks

This query returns a list of notebooks and how many DBUs each notebook consumed, in descending order by DBU consumption

SELECT
  usage_metadata.notebook_id,
  usage_metadata. notebook_path,
  SUM(usage_quantity) as total_dbu
FROM
  system.billing.usage
WHERE
  usage_metadata.notebook_id is not null
  and billing_origin_product = 'INTERACTIVE'
  and product_features.is_serverless
  and usage_unit = 'DBU'
  and usage_date >= DATEADD(day, -30, current_date)
GROUP BY
  1,2
ORDER BY
  total_dbu DESC

Identify expensive jobs

This query returns a list of jobs and how many DBUs each job consumed, in descending order by DBU consumption:

SELECT
  usage_metadata.job_id,
  usage_metadata.job_name,
  SUM(usage_quantity) as total_dbu
FROM
  system.billing.usage
WHERE
  usage_metadata.job_id is not null
  and usage_unit = 'DBU'
  and usage_date >= DATEADD(day, -30, current_date)
  and sku_name like '%JOBS_SERVERLESS_COMPUTE%'
GROUP BY
  1,2
ORDER BY
  total_dbu DESC

Report on DBUs consumed by a particular user

This query returns a list of notebooks and jobs run by a particular user or service principal, and the number of DBUs consumed by each workload. Be sure to insert a valid user email address using the parameters.

SELECT
  usage_metadata.job_id,
  usage_metadata.job_name,
  usage_metadata.notebook_id,
  usage_metadata. notebook_path,
  SUM(usage_quantity) as total_dbu
FROM
  system.billing.usage
WHERE
  identity_metadata.run_as = format_string('%s@%s.%s', :user_name, :domain_name, :top_level_domain)
  and usage_date >= DATEADD(day, -30, current_date)
GROUP BY
  1,2,3,4
ORDER BY
  total_dbu DESC

Report on DBUs consumed by workloads that share a custom tag

This query returns a list of jobs that share the same custom tag, and the number of DBUs consumed by each workload:

SELECT
  usage_metadata.job_id,
  usage_metadata.job_name,
  usage_metadata.notebook_id,
  usage_metadata. notebook_path,
  SUM(usage_quantity) as total_dbu
FROM
  system.billing.usage
WHERE
  custom_tags.<key> = '<value>'
  and billing_origin_product in ('JOBS','INTERACTIVE')
  and product_features.is_serverless
  and usage_unit = 'DBU'
  and usage_date >= DATEADD(day, -30, current_date)
GROUP BY
  1,2,3,4
ORDER BY
  total_dbu DESC

What are the top 10 tables accessed by user yogananda.muthaiah@sap.com?

SELECT
    IFNULL(request_params.full_name_arg, 'Non-specific') AS `TABLE ACCESSED`
FROM system.access.audit
WHERE user_identity.email = 'yogananda.muthaiah@sap.com'
    AND action_name IN ('commandSubmit','getTable')
GROUP BY IFNULL(request_params.full_name_arg, 'Non-specific')
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 10

Who are the top 10 users who access table catalog.schema.table?

This query identifies which are the top 10 users accessing table catalog.schema.table.

SELECT
    COUNT(*),
    user_identity.email
FROM
    system.access.audit
WHERE
    action_name IN ('commandSubmit', 'getTable')
    AND request_params.full_name_arg = 'catalog.schema.table'
GROUP BY user_identity.email
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 10

What has this user accessed in the last 24 hours?

This query identifies which tables user yogananda.muthaiah@sap.com accessed in the last 24 hours

SELECT
    IFNULL(request_params.full_name_arg, 'Non-specific') AS `table_accessed`
FROM system.access.audit
WHERE user_identity.email = 'yogananda.muthaiah@sap.com'
    AND action_name IN ('commandSubmit','getTable')
    AND event_time >= NOW() - '1 day'::INTERVAL
GROUP BY IFNULL(request_params.full_name_arg, 'Non-specific')
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC

entry.title: Re: Change Super Administrator SAP for me
entry.content:
Normally, the current Super user in your company can set the other user as the new Super User using Copy User's Authorization feature. After review, I can confirm that there is one active Super Administrator for your company who is listed on Users & Contacts - SAP for Me. Therefore, could you please contact the existing Super Administrator to perform this action? 

  • Add Super Administrator - An existing Super Administrator must provide all authorizations to another S-user ID.
    Tip: Follow KBA 1614304 to copy or clone their own authorizations.

For more detailed information, please refer to the following KBAs:

2596214 - How to maintain Super, Cloud, and User Administrator S-user IDs listed in My Important Contacts - SAP for Me - SAP for Me 

1282808 - What tasks is a Super, Cloud, or User Administrator S-user ID responsible for? - SAP for Me - SAP for Me

If you have any questions or need further assistance, please feel free to contact us.

Best regards,
Judy
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: How can I prevent certain users from executing report...
entry.content:
Hi Joerg,

helping  you is like an old chewing gum.

So I understand you right, you have a Layout Definition and choose there the tab permissions.

Is this Layout a Select Grid ?

Is there a Printdefinition  before the Layout?

Is the Layout Definition bound to the SAP-Menu?

I tried it with a Layout bound to the menu and the report was not visible anymore.

At least you can setup a UserGroup or additional authorisation.

Use the Document - Generate Script in the Layout

There you can querie the authorisation  itself by LD.QueryData

The user is a given parameter in layouts

If the user is not allowed you can Engine.CancelRender and the Layout stops rendering and won't be opened.

Regards Lothar

entry.title: How to verify email delivery for invoices in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

we are sending invoices via email from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

My question is: How can I verify that the email was actually delivered to the recipient?

For example, it could happen that the email address stored for the customer is no longer valid or does not exist anymore.

I am aware of the app Monitoring Email Transmissions and already tested sending an invoice to a deliberately incorrect email address. In the app, the transmission still shows the status "OK", even though the email address should not exist.

Because of this, I am unsure what exactly the status OK represents.

  • Does it only mean that the email was successfully handed over to the mail server?

  • Or is there any way in S/4HANA Cloud to confirm that the email was actually delivered to the recipient's mailbox?

  • Is there any monitoring or bounce-handling mechanism available?

Thanks in advance!

Kathi

entry.title: Transport changes not available in QA but successful in PRD
entry.content:
Good day All

I am experiencing a strange issue with transports, which after a few days of investigation is still not sorted.

 

Steps:

1. Transports are created in the DEV system

2. Import transports to QA system, STMS shows successful, no error logs

3. Check changes in QA system and it is not available

4. Import the same transports to PRD system successfully - All changes available

 

Extra details:

S/4HANA 2023 On Premise

- These are HR related transports with tables and views and also related to transaction PE01 PE02 PE03

- HR patching was done 1 month ago - All systems on same component levels

- QA refresh was done 1 week ago

 

Workaround: What I did notice is that if you open the QA system for changes(SE06) and import the transports then the changes show in the QA system.

 

This is my last option before opening a case with SAP, which hopefully is not needed.

Regards

Werner

entry.title: Re: Journal Entry Number Range From Number Update

Good day @ACLyn 

Thank you for your question.

In practice, once you have used the journal entry number for postings in the fiscal year, the “From Number” field is generally no longer editable. That is why it appears greyed out in the app.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/4460d7531a4d424de10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/1fe9ab02f1114619a13a7df8ed87ab9b.html?locale=en-US

I suggest you take the path of reviewing the current interval design and if you require, consider maintaining a valid alternative configuration in your configuration environment.

Chris

entry.title: Key User Adaptation: Powerful New Features & Seamless Transition to Developer Extensibility
entry.content:
With key user adaptation, you can change the user interface of SAP Fiori apps directly in the Fiori launchpad — intuitively and without having to write new code. The changes are upgrade-safe adaptations to SAP Fiori apps. Key users can open the UI Adaptation mode for a Fiori app directly from their user menu in the Fiori launchpad (menu entry Adapt UI).

A key user is a business expert who is authorized to make changes on behalf of other users — for example a team member who is interested in technology. The goal of key user extensibility is to empower business users to be able to adapt their working environment to suit their needs, without having to rely on system administrators.

 

What can key users change with key user adaptation?

Key users can perform the following changes in Fiori apps based on SAP Fiori elements:

  • Hide/show the information that shall be visible in the object page header
  • Hide/show tabs, sections, tables, or action buttons on the object page
  • Define the fields visible in forms on the object page
  • Set the configuration for tables (select all, selection mode, thresholds, view settings, export, etc.)
  • Set the configuration for the filter bar (clear button, usage of the semantic date range control)
  • Set the configuration for the complete list report / object page (variant management, initial load)
  • Change Text Arrangement (ID only, Text first, Text last, Text only)
  • Change Labels (global label changes)
  • Rename (only for a dedicated field – applies only locally to a form field)
  • Define the fields visible in the mass edit dialog. In the key user adaptation mode, all fields except static read-only fields are available.
  • And more

The scope was continuously enhanced. The bold items above are extremely powerful new features available since S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2508 / S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 / On-Premise 2025. They are described in detail in the following sections.

 

New feature: configuration for tables

The Configuration functionality is available in key user adaptation mode when the context menu entry Configuration is selected on a table, on the filter bar, or on page level. The context menu on a table contains — for example in Fiori elements V4 — the following entries:

 

Among other things, the following settings can be configured here:

  • Enable Table Export — configures whether the Export button is displayed in the table.
  • Frozen Column Count — configures the count of columns that are always visible when scrolling horizontally.
  • Personalization — configures whether the table personalization settings are enabled. To configure the listed settings individually, choose Own Settings.
  • Row Count — configures the number of rows to be displayed in the table.
  • Width Including Column Header — configures whether the column header label is considered when calculating the column width.
  • Enable Select All — configures whether the Select All option is displayed in the table.
  • Selection Limit — configures the maximum limit of the number of rows that can be selected at once (e.g., by using Select All).
  • Threshold — determines the maximum number of entries loaded when the application is started. Scroll Threshold determines the maximum number loaded during scrolling if new entries need to be fetched. Increasing these settings will lead to an increased initial load time but deliver a smoother scroll experience.

Note: Not all settings listed above are available for all table types and page types. Furthermore, there are differences between Fiori elements V2 and Fiori elements V4 (Fiori elements V2 offers fewer settings).

Example – List Report Grid Table in Fiori elements V4:

 

 

New feature: configuration for the filter bar

The Configuration context menu entry on the filter bar opens settings for the filter bar. Among other things, the following settings can be configured here:

  • Display Clear Button — configures whether the Clear button is displayed in the filter bar.
  • Enable Semantic Date Range menu options — configures that single date range filters offer semantic operators (like "Today", "This Month", "Last Year").

Example – List Report Filter Bar in Fiori elements V4:

  

 

New feature: configuration for the complete list report / object page

The Configuration context menu entry at page level opens settings for the entire List Report or Object Page. Among other things, the following settings can be configured here:

  • Variant Management (Table Variant Management) — configures how variant management is used on the List Report page or in tables on the Object Page.
  • Initial Load — configures how data is loaded initially when the app is opened.

Example – List Report in Fiori elements V4:

 

 

New feature: change text arrangement

The Change Text Arrangement functionality is available in key user adaptation mode when the context menu entry Change Text Arrangement is selected on a table, on the filter bar, or on a form.

This powerful new functionality allows changing centrally the text arrangement for all relevant fields in a Fiori app. A key user can decide for each field whether it shall be shown as:

Option

Example (Company Code "0001" / "SAP AG")

ID only

0001

Text only

SAP AG

Text first

SAP AG (0001)

Text last

0001 (SAP AG)

Note: These changes are independent of specific controls. You change the text arrangement for the underlying OData property. If a specific field or control does not use the text arrangement from the OData property, the changes will not be applied to that control.

 

 

New feature: change labels

The Change Labels functionality is available in key user adaptation mode when the context menu entry Change Labels is selected on a table, on the filter bar, or on a form.

This allows changing the labels centrally for all relevant fields in a Fiori app: if an identical label is available in the filter bar, in tables, forms, and charts of a Fiori application, then all places are changed consistently. This is the key difference from the existing Rename feature, which only applies locally to a form field.

Example: You can consistently change the text "Material" to "Product" across your entire Fiori app.

Note: These changes are independent of specific controls. You change the label for the underlying OData property. If a specific field or control does not use the text from the OData property, the changes will not be applied to that control.

 

 

Need more complex changes? Seamless Transition from Key User to Developer Extensibility.

If you need to make more complex changes — for example, adding fragments to visualize data as a chart, changing a responsive table to a grid table, or making the app available to different business roles, user groups, organizational units, and countries — you can extend an application by creating an SAPUI5 Adaptation Project (developer adaptation).

When creating an SAPUI5 Adaptation Project, you can select the brand-new option Import Key User Changes (available since S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602; not yet available for S/4HANA Private Cloud / On-Premise) to import all key user changes made so far. This means key user changes will not be lost — they become part of the new SAPUI5 Adaptation Project.

 

For an introduction to Adaptation Projects, see Work with SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects to Make Changes and Extend the Source Code of an Application Variant of an SAP Fiori Application in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

For more detailed information, see Extending SAP Fiori Applications.

 

Prerequisites

Public Cloud:

  • The IAM app F6184_TRAN provides the relevant authorizations. It is included in the business catalogs SAP_CORE_BC_EXT_FLX_CUS_PC and SAP_CORE_BC_EXT_FLX_PC (see Adapting SAP Fiori UIs at Runtime - Key User Adaptation).

Private Cloud / On-Premise:

  • Your administrator assigned you to the authorization role SAP_UI_FLEX_KEY_USER (see Enabling Key User Adaptation).

General requirements:

  • You run your SAPUI5 app on a desktop computer or laptop.
  • Your app is enabled for key user adaptation.
  • A minimum screen size of 800×600 is required for key user adaptation. Anything smaller can affect the toolbar elements, causing them to overlap.

 

Tips for Key User Adaptation for Fiori Apps in SAP S/4HANA

Where do I make Key User changes?

  • Key users typically make changes in the Development environment.

How do I transport Key User changes?

  • Active changes must be published to make them ready for transport.
  • To publish your changes for transport, you must be assigned a transport request by your technical team.
  • Your technical team manages the transports through to your Production environment.

 

Key takeaways

  • Key User Adaptation lets you make common changes to SAP Fiori apps directly in the Fiori Launchpad — no developer knowledge required.
  • Key User Adaptation is now extremely powerful with new options for:
    • Configuring important UI elements (tables, filter bar, complete list report / object page)
    • Changing the text arrangement of fields
    • Changing labels globally across an entire Fiori app
  • Changes are made in the Development environment and published for transport.
  • Published changes can be transported to other environments by your technical team.
  • Key User Adaptation changes can be imported when creating an SAPUI5 Adaptation Project for more complex changes (S/4HANA Public Cloud 2602+). This allows a seamless transition from key user to developer extensibility.

 

Where can I learn more about Key User Adaptation for Fiori Apps in SAP S/4HANA?

  • SAP Help – Key User Adaptation (S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition): Adapting SAP Fiori UIs at Runtime – Key User Adaptation
  • SAP Help – Key User Adaptation (S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and On-Premise): Adapting SAP Fiori UIs at Runtime – Key User Adaptation
  • SAP Learning – Tutorials: Key User Adaptation for SAPUI5 Applications
  • SAP Help – SAP Fiori elements V4: Extending Delivered Apps With Key User Adaptation
  • SAP Help – SAP Fiori elements V2: Adapting the UI

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to have different vendor on unplanned...
entry.content:
Hello @Fidelis 

Yes, you can do that using the Create Supplier Invoice-Advanced app. See KBA 3426129 and Entering a Supplier Invoice for more insights.

Regarding your question, can the unplanned delivery cost be posted as part of the material moving average pice:

Yes, the Public Cloud provides this option. However, it depends on how you configure unplanned delivery costs. You can decide whether the unplanned delivery cost is distributed to invoice items or posted to a separate accounting line. If you distribute the cost to stock relevant invoice items for a material that is managed with moving average price, the material valuation is updated accordingly.

Consult these references for more details on unplanned delivery cost distribution:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/634261119fec4d58970471f2c4a9a740/8daa3fa8103347ef9830a8337f69f3bd.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/b6c85168201041aaa97bd8e3cb0d9e28.html?locale=en-US

Note that, if your business scenario entails a recurring freight vendor different from the PO vendor, then you should handle such cleanly through planned delivery costs in the purchase order rather than relying on unplanned delivery costs at the time of the invoice. KBA 3548380 highlights the behaviour of delivery cost and its dependency on the freight condition setup.

If your business requirement is only to allow the freight invoice to come from a different vendor and still have the cost absorbed into the material value, then the recommended standard approach is for you to:

  • Use Create Supplier Invoice-Advanced app
  • Enter the relevant PO reference
  • Maintain the different Invoicing Party
  • Ensure you set configuration 101948 so that unplanned delivery costs are distributed to invoice items rather than posted separately.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: SAP Currency conversion rate checking for a particula...
entry.content:
Hi @KVKUMAR ,
SAP stores exchange rates in table TCURR, and the standard way to view or maintain them is through the following transactions:T‑Code: OB08 — Maintain Exchange Rates
This is the most common screen people use. Enter T‑code: OB08 and Press Enter then you will view exchange rates by: Exchange rate type (e.g., M = average rate), From currency / To currency and valid-from date. Check the exact rate used on the date your PR was created.

entry.title: Re: The returns indicator cannot be set for the purchase...
entry.content:
Hi @singh-siddhant ,
My friend you are seeing this error because SAP ECC is blocking the RETPO (returns indicator) for your STO item based on configuration or item type restrictions. The issue is common in intercompany Return STO scenarios and usually comes down to missing configuration or an item category that does not allow returns. Below is a clear, structured way to diagnose and fix it.

Item Category Does Not Allow Returns. SAP Help explicitly states that RETPO cannot be set for:
Service items (Item category D)
Limit items (Item category B)

check in ME21N/ME22N → Item details → Item category. Ensure it is Standard (blank) and not D or B. To Fix change item category to standard Or create a new STO with a standard item category

entry.title: Re: Cash Management Planning & Reporting Requirements for...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for your question.

Reporting project-level cash flow (inflows and outflows) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, similar to the CJIA (Project Actual Payment Line Items) report in on-premise, can be achieved using Embedded Analytics with Custom CDS Views. However, there is no standard delivered report in Public Cloud that provides detailed cash flow reporting at the project level.

Standard apps like Project Cost Report (F2513) support plan vs. actual cost analysis and line-item drill-down, but they are limited to cost reporting and do not provide cash flow (payment-based) visibility.

 

To meet this requirement, the recommended approach is to build a custom analytical report using Embedded SAC, combining Finance (Cash Management) data with Project (EPPM) information.

The typical solution involves the following steps.

1. Ensure project assignment in financial postings
You should ensure that all relevant postings carry Project / WBS Element information, including:
Supplier invoices
Customer invoices
Payments
Journal entries

This is essential because:
Cash flow is derived from FI postings, not directly from projects

2. Configure Liquidity Items (Cash Flow Driver)
Cash flow in S/4HANA Cloud is managed via Liquidity Items in Cash Management.
You should:
Assign liquidity items to G/L accounts
Maintain derivation rules based on:
G/L account
Document type
This enables classification of postings into:
Cash Inflow
Cash Outflow

3. Create a Custom CDS View using the Custom CDS Views app
You can use finance-related data sources such as:
I_JournalEntryItem – Journal Entry Line Items
This provides the base data for actual cash movements.
You can also add associations such as:
I_WBSElement – for WBS/Project details
I_Project – for project master data


With this approach, customers can monitor:
Actual cash inflow and outflow per project
Net cash position
Time-based cash movement
CJIA-like payment visibility


Important Notes:
App Project Cost Report (F2513) and Project Cost Report – Line Items (F2538) are the closest standard apps to CJI3 (Actual cost line items)

These apps are cost/revenue-based, not payment-based
Didnt found CJIA (actual payment line items) in standard Public Cloud
Cash flow is handled via Cash Management (Liquidity Items / One Exposure)


With this approach, you can replicate CJIA-like functionality in a cloud-compliant way by combining cash flow data with project assignments.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards
HM Awasthi

entry.title: Re: Change batch of sales order
entry.content:
Hello @li_zhang8ed3ad 

Thank you for your question.

Your research is correct. No, you cannot change the batch number of sales order items by using the Mass Change of Sales Documents app. The mass change functions SAP provide does not include batch as an item change option.

See the following reference for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/315edd1c8c1f4675bc68bee977849198.html?locale=en-US

If you need to influence or reselect the batch after you have created a sales order, you will have to handle this through the batch determination process in the sales order or delivery flow, not through mass maintenance. See KBA 3649063 and KBA 3267138 for more insights on supported batch determination fields and its limitations.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Credit check should not happen again, once Sale Order is released
entry.content:
I am creating a sale Order for quantity 10. But the confirmed quantity is 5. Credit check is happening against the Confirmed quantity 5 and blocking the SO. Then the SO is released using VKM3/VKM4. Then while altering the Quantity above 5 or altering the schedule line date, the order is getting Credit blocked again.

To prevent this we tried altering the LV_AMOUNT in  BADI_SD_CM -> Method FSCM_CREDIT_CHECK_ORDER to take the Total Order amount (Logic=  Net Value + Tax ). Even after this, the old behavior based on Confirmed quantity is happening. Can anyone guide how to achieve the above requirement. Also the Credit exposure for the BP has to be updated based on the Order Quantity.

entry.title: Option to disable the REHIRE for ONB, while keeping it active for EC
entry.content:
The customer wants to disable the Rehire process for Onboarding, but keep it for Employee Central.

The set up i know is that Rehire is enabled in Manager Data in the same admin tool as for EC. There is no switch in the system to disable it for ONB module, but keep for EC. Even if all the permissions are removed and there are no users in Rehire dynamic group - the Rehire process keeps triggering for ONB and blocking the process if noone can see the Verification step.

Is there any known set up or workaround to Disable the Rehire just of ONB module?

 

Thank you,

JS

entry.title: Re: Option to disable the REHIRE for ONB, while keeping i...
entry.content:
@YanaStupane Please refer below KBA:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3099039 - How to skip Rehire Verification Process in Onboarding without impacting EC? - Onboarding

NOTE:  This is not a recommended practice and only a suggestion for what can be tried by the customer/partner in their preview environments. The potential issue would be that system will not recognize the rehires in onboarding and would get blocked at Manage Pending Hire with a duplicate national ID, end up hiring someone who is a rehire as a new hire, or even candidate not appearing on Manage Pending Hire. The National Id duplicate check in Manage Pending Hire will trigger despite candidates rehire verification skipping during the onboarding process.

Thanks,

Gopi
